payload.description: Demonstration program imposing owner liability for failure of operator to comply with posted maximum speed limits in highway construction or maintenance work areas on up to nine TBTA Authority facilities, using radar and automated cameras

evidence_refs.source_quote: Work Zone Speed Enforcement (WZSE) Program

demonstration program imposing owner liability for failure of an operator to comply with certain posted maximum speed limits in highway construction or maintenance work areas on up to nine Authority facilities, also known as the Work Zone Speed Enforcement Program (WZSE)

payload.claim_text: WZSE has been shown to deter speeding in maintenance and construction zones, enhancing worker and traveler safety
payload.subject: Work Zone Speed Enforcement
payload.description: Claim that WZSE deters speeding based on NYSDOT and Connecticut examples

evidence_refs.source_quote: WZSE has been shown to deter speeding in maintenance and construction zones, enhancing worker and traveler safety

payload.claim_text: TBTA's proposed WZSE program will use technology, including radar and automated cameras, to identify vehicles driving ten miles per hour or more above the posted speed limit in an active work zone
payload.subject: TBTA WZSE Program
payload.description: Technology used for WZSE enforcement

evidence_refs.source_quote: TBTA's proposed WZSE program will use technology, including radar and automated cameras, to identify vehicles driving ten miles per hour or more above the posted speed limit in an active work zone

tool_args.label: WZSE cameras operate only when workers present

payload.claim_text: The radar and cameras will only operate in work zones when workers are present
payload.subject: TBTA WZSE Program
payload.description: Operating condition for WZSE equipment

evidence_refs.source_quote: The radar and cameras will only operate in work zones when workers are present

payload.claim_text: Vehicle registrants will receive a notice of liability with fine amounts ranging from $50 to $100
payload.subject: WZSE fines
payload.description: Fine range for WZSE violations

evidence_refs.source_quote: fine amounts ranging from $50 to $100

payload.claim_text: TBTA will receive 80% of the fines collected. After paying operating costs, 60% of the remaining funds are to be allocated to work zone safety projects
payload.subject: WZSE revenue allocation
payload.description: Distribution of fine revenue from WZSE program

evidence_refs.source_quote: TBTA will receive 80% of the fines collected. After paying operating costs, 60% of the remaining funds are to be allocated to work zone safety projects
